Method.doc:
Initialize the WindowQuickJump spoon

Parameters:
 * None

Returns:
 * The WindowQuickJump object
Method.name: init

Method.doc:
Start the WindowQuickJump spoon

Parameters:
 * None

Returns:
 * The WindowQuickJump object

Notes:
 * This method initializes the spoon but doesn't show badges
 * Use the hotkey to toggle badge visibility
Method.name: start

Method.doc:
Stop the WindowQuickJump spoon

Parameters:
 * None

Returns:
 * The WindowQuickJump object

Notes:
 * Hides any visible badges and unbinds hotkeys
Method.name: stop

Method.doc:
Bind hotkeys for WindowQuickJump

Parameters:
 * mapping - A table with hotkey mappings:
   * toggle - hotkey to show/hide window badges (e.g., {{"cmd", "alt"}, "j"})

Returns:
 * The WindowQuickJump object

Notes:
 * Only the 'toggle' key is used to show/hide badges
 * While badges are shown, press number/letter keys to jump to windows
 * Press Escape to cancel
Method.name: bindHotkeys
parameters: * mapping - A table with hotkey mappings

Method.desc: Toggle the visibility of window badges
Method.doc:
Toggle the visibility of window badges

Parameters:
 * None

Returns:
 * The WindowQuickJump object

Notes:
 * Shows numbered/lettered badges on all visible windows
 * Badges use keys 1-9 and A-Z for up to 35 windows
 * Press the corresponding key to focus that window
 * Press Escape to cancel
Method.name: toggle

Variable.desc: Maximum number of windows to show badges for
Variable.doc: Maximum number of windows to show badges for (default: 35)
Variable.name: maxWindows

Variable.desc: Size of the badge circle in pixels
Variable.doc: Size of the badge circle in pixels (default: 28)
Variable.name: badgeSize

Variable.desc: Maximum width for window title display
Variable.doc: Maximum width for window title display in pixels (default: 440)
Variable.name: titleMaxWidth

Variable.desc: Padding from window edges for badge placement
Variable.doc: Padding from window edges for badge placement in pixels (default: 6)
Variable.name: badgePadding

Variable.doc: Logger instance for debugging. Set logger.level to "debug" for verbose output.
Variable.name: logger

desc: Window Quick Jump — numeric/alpha badges to quickly change focus between windows
doc:
Window Quick Jump — numeric/alpha badges to quickly change focus between windows

This Spoon provides a visual method to quickly switch between windows by showing
keyboard badges (1-9, A-Z) on all visible windows. Press the hotkey to show badges,
then press the corresponding key to focus that window.

Download: [https://github.com/AndrewDryga/spoons/raw/main/WindowQuickJump.spoon.zip](https://github.com/AndrewDryga/spoons/raw/main/WindowQuickJump.spoon.zip)

Features:
 * Visual badges with numbers and letters for up to 35 windows
 * Smart positioning to prevent badge overlap
 * Dark mode support with automatic theme detection
 * Active window highlighting
 * Window title display with app names
 * Minimized and hidden windows are automatically skipped

Example configuration:
```lua
hs.loadSpoon("WindowQuickJump")

-- Customize appearance (optional)
spoon.WindowQuickJump.maxWindows = 35      -- Maximum windows to badge
spoon.WindowQuickJump.badgeSize = 28       -- Badge circle size
spoon.WindowQuickJump.badgeFontSize = 15   -- Font size
spoon.WindowQuickJump.titleMaxWidth = 440  -- Max title width
spoon.WindowQuickJump.badgePadding = 6     -- Edge padding

-- Bind hotkey
spoon.WindowQuickJump:bindHotkeys({
    toggle = {{"cmd", "alt"}, "j"}
})

spoon.WindowQuickJump:start()
```

Usage:
1. Press your hotkey (e.g., Cmd+Alt+J) to show badges
2. Press the number or letter shown on the window you want
3. Press Escape to cancel without switching
